moments.label: Starting to escalate
moments.description: The child is upset and warning signs are showing, but no one is actively being hurt yet.

moments.label: Unsafe right now
moments.description: Someone, an animal, the child’s body, or the environment may be hurt right now.

moments.label: After it happened
moments.description: Everyone is physically safe and the parent needs repair, accountability, or a next-time plan.

age_bands.note: Use very few words. The adult controls the environment and offers one safe body action.
Use short, concrete words. Give one clear boundary and one replacement action.
Preserve dignity while setting a firm line. Offer space, words, or a specific reset.

age_bands.label: Teen
age_bands.note: Use respectful, non-escalating language. Prioritize distance, support, and safety over control.

show_another_phrase_rule.description: Rotate among approved cards with the same behavior_id, age_band_id, and moment_id. Never call live AI.
show_another_phrase_rule.fallback: If a combo ever has fewer than two approved cards, hide or disable Show another phrase.

search_rule.description: Search/classification retrieves existing approved cards only. It must not generate new scripts or advice.
search_rule.hidden_search_terms_note: Hidden search terms may include words parents type, but displayed script fields must stay universal.

universal_language_rule.description: User-facing script fields must work for any household, child, bystander, animal, school, or public setting without requiring the parent to translate relationship words during a hard moment.

cards.say_this: Your body is getting mad. I won’t let you hit or kick. Hands can squeeze this, or feet can stomp the floor.
cards.do_this: Point to one safe place for the anger to go: a pillow, the floor, or space away from people.
cards.helpful_note: Very young children need the adult to manage the space first. The words support the action; they do not replace it.

cards.say_this: Big feelings are okay. Hitting and kicking are not okay. Hands can squeeze this, or feet can stomp the floor.
cards.do_this: Move close enough to supervise, but not so close that your body becomes the target. Use fewer than ten words.
cards.helpful_note: Too many words can feel like more pressure. This gives the child a way to stay connected without continuing the unsafe behavior.

cards.say_this: I’m here. I won’t let you hit or kick. I’m helping your body get safe.
cards.do_this: Offer one safe body action and remove the most tempting unsafe item or target.
cards.helpful_note: This keeps the parent from entering a power struggle. The message is calm, clear, and repeatable.

cards.say_this: I won’t let you hit or kick. I’m moving everyone back. Everyone is staying safe.
cards.do_this: Move everyone back and keep your body out of striking range.
cards.helpful_note: At this age, safety is almost entirely an adult job. Keep the words simple and change the environment quickly.
cards.safety_note: If the unsafe behavior continues or someone may be seriously hurt, get immediate in-person help.

cards.say_this: Stop. Bodies safe. I’m making space now.
cards.do_this: Use your body to create space and keep your words short. Do not try to get an explanation.
cards.helpful_note: Repeating one safety phrase helps the parent avoid fueling the escalation with debate, questions, or lectures.
cards.safety_note: If the unsafe behavior continues or someone may be seriously hurt, get immediate in-person help.

cards.say_this: I won’t let anyone get hurt. I’m helping your body move away.
cards.do_this: Stay nearby if safe, but make the area boring, open, and lower stimulation.
cards.helpful_note: The priority is reducing access to people, animals, objects, or unsafe spaces, not getting the child to agree with the boundary.
cards.safety_note: If the unsafe behavior continues or someone may be seriously hurt, get immediate in-person help.

cards.say_this: You were mad. Hitting and kicking hurt people. Let’s help make it better.
cards.do_this: Help the child check on the person affected, then practice one safer body action.
cards.helpful_note: Tiny repair teaches connection without expecting adult-level insight. Keep it warm and very simple.

cards.say_this: That was too much for your body. Now we help fix what we can.
cards.do_this: Choose one tiny repair, such as checking on the person, getting an ice pack, or practicing hands on a pillow. Do it with the child instead of sending them away alone.
cards.helpful_note: A concrete repair works better than a forced apology. The child learns what to do after harm happens.

cards.say_this: The unsafe part is over. Now we do a small helper job.
cards.do_this: Practice the safer body action for five seconds and then reconnect with a calm routine.
cards.helpful_note: The next-time plan matters because the child needs a replacement action they can actually access under stress.

cards.say_this: I can see this is getting too big. I won’t let you hit or kick. Hands on the pillow or feet on the floor.
cards.do_this: Point to one safe place for the anger to go: a pillow, the floor, or space away from people.
cards.helpful_note: The goal is to interrupt the pattern before the body goes fully unsafe. A short boundary plus one next step is easier to use than a long explanation.

cards.say_this: Big feelings are okay. Hitting and kicking are not okay. Hands on the pillow or feet on the floor.
cards.do_this: Lower your voice, reduce eye contact if it increases pressure, and give only one next step.
cards.helpful_note: Too many words can feel like more pressure. This gives the child a way to stay connected without continuing the unsafe behavior.

cards.say_this: Pause. Your body needs a safer choice. Hands on the pillow or feet on the floor.
cards.do_this: Create a little physical space and repeat the same calm phrase instead of adding new instructions.
cards.helpful_note: This keeps the parent from entering a power struggle. The message is calm, clear, and repeatable.

cards.say_this: I’m moving everyone back. I won’t let anyone get hurt. We’ll talk when bodies are safe.
cards.do_this: Move everyone back and keep your body out of striking range.
cards.helpful_note: Safety comes before teaching. The repair conversation can happen after bodies and the environment are safe.
cards.safety_note: If the unsafe behavior continues or someone may be seriously hurt, get immediate in-person help.

cards.say_this: This is not safe. I’m making space between bodies now.
cards.do_this: Stop teaching, questioning, or correcting. Use one safety phrase and focus on distance.
cards.helpful_note: Repeating one safety phrase helps the parent avoid fueling the escalation with debate, questions, or lectures.
cards.safety_note: If the unsafe behavior continues or someone may be seriously hurt, get immediate in-person help.

cards.say_this: I won’t argue while someone could get hurt. Bodies safe first, talking after.
cards.do_this: Move vulnerable people, animals, and breakable or dangerous items away before trying to discuss what happened.
cards.helpful_note: The priority is reducing access to people, animals, objects, or unsafe spaces, not getting the child to agree with the boundary.
cards.safety_note: If the unsafe behavior continues or someone may be seriously hurt, get immediate in-person help.

cards.say_this: You were angry, and hitting and kicking hurt people. We are going to repair it and practice what to do next time.
cards.do_this: Help the child check on the person affected, then practice one safer body action.
cards.helpful_note: Repair builds responsibility without shame. The feeling can be valid while the unsafe behavior remains unacceptable.

cards.say_this: That got too big. We need to make it right and choose a safer plan.
cards.do_this: Choose one concrete repair, such as checking on the person, getting an ice pack, or practicing hands on a pillow. Keep it brief and doable.
cards.helpful_note: A concrete repair works better than a forced apology. The child learns what to do after harm happens.

cards.say_this: Your feeling was okay. The unsafe choice was not. Let’s repair and practice.
cards.do_this: Practice the next-time action once while everyone is calm, then end the conversation before it becomes another lecture.
cards.helpful_note: The next-time plan matters because the child needs a replacement action they can actually access under stress.

cards.do_this: Point to one safe place for the anger to go: a pillow, the floor, or space away from people.

cards.say_this: This is the point where we pause. Take space or use words from over there, and I’ll listen when bodies are safe.
cards.do_this: Create a little physical space and repeat the same calm phrase instead of adding new instructions.
cards.helpful_note: This keeps the parent from entering a power struggle. The message is calm, clear, and repeatable.

cards.say_this: Being angry made sense. Hitting and kicking still crossed a safety line. We need to repair what we can and make a safer plan.

cards.say_this: We’re not going to pretend this did not happen. We’re going to repair what we can and plan for next time.

cards.do_this: Practice the next-time action once while everyone is calm, then end the conversation before it becomes another lecture.
cards.helpful_note: The next-time plan matters because the child needs a replacement action they can actually access under stress.

cards.say_this: I want to hear you, but I won’t stay in a moment where people may get hurt. Take space now, and we can try again when bodies are safe.
cards.do_this: Point to one safe place for the anger to go: a pillow, the floor, or space away from people.

cards.say_this: Big feelings can be real. Hitting and kicking are not okay. Take space now, and we can try again when bodies are safe.
cards.do_this: Step back and do not follow, corner, block, or try to force eye contact. Keep the exit path safe.

cards.say_this: This is escalating. I’m pausing the conversation before it becomes unsafe. Take space now, and we can try again when bodies are safe.
cards.do_this: Name the pause, then stop talking long enough for the intensity to drop.

cards.say_this: I’m creating space now. I’m not going to fight with you. I’m going to keep people safe.

cards.say_this: This is unsafe. I’m stepping away and getting support if we need it.
